name: proxmark3
title: proxmark3

commands.name: proxmark3

arguments.description: this help
arguments.raw: -h/--help                           this help

arguments.description: print client version
arguments.raw: -v/--version                        print client version

arguments.description: serial port to connect to
arguments.raw: -p/--port                           serial port to connect to

arguments.description: 20sec waiting the serial port to appear in the OS
arguments.raw: -w/--wait                           20sec waiting the serial port to appear in the OS

arguments.description: output will be flushed after every print
arguments.raw: -f/--flush                          output will be flushed after every print

arguments.description: set debugmode
arguments.raw: -d/--debug <0|1|2>                  set debugmode

arguments.description: dump all interactive command list at once
arguments.raw: -t/--text                           dump all interactive command list at once

arguments.description: dump all interactive command's help at once
arguments.raw: --fulltext                          dump all interactive command's help at once

arguments.description: dump all interactive command list at once in markdown syntax
arguments.raw: -m/--markdown                       dump all interactive command list at once in markdown syntax

arguments.description: serial port speed (only needed for physical UART, not for USB-CDC or BT)
arguments.raw: -b/--baud                           serial port speed (only needed for physical UART, not for USB-CDC or BT)

arguments.description: execute one Proxmark3 command (or several separated by ';').
arguments.raw: -c/--command <command>              execute one Proxmark3 command (or several separated by ';').

arguments.description: execute Lua script.
arguments.raw: -l/--lua <lua_script_file>          execute Lua script.

arguments.description: execute Python script.
arguments.raw: -y/--py <python_script_file>        execute Python script.

arguments.description: script file with one Proxmark3 command per line
arguments.raw: -s/--script-file <cmd_script_file>  script file with one Proxmark3 command per line

arguments.description: enter interactive mode after executing the script or the command
arguments.raw: -i/--interactive                    enter interactive mode after executing the script or the command

arguments.description: do not use history, prefs file nor log files
arguments.raw: --incognito                         do not use history, prefs file nor log files

arguments.description: override number of CPU cores
arguments.raw: --ncpu <num_cores>                  override number of CPU cores

arguments.description: flash Proxmark3, requires at least one --image
arguments.raw: --flash                             flash Proxmark3, requires at least one --image

arguments.description: reboot Proxmark3 into bootloader mode
arguments.raw: --reboot-to-bootloader              reboot Proxmark3 into bootloader mode

arguments.description: Enable flashing of bootloader area *DANGEROUS* (need --flash)
arguments.raw: --unlock-bootloader                 Enable flashing of bootloader area *DANGEROUS* (need --flash)

arguments.description: Enable flashing even if firmware seems to not match client version
arguments.raw: --force                             Enable flashing even if firmware seems to not match client version

arguments.description: image to flash. Can be specified several times.
arguments.raw: --image <imagefile>                 image to flash. Can be specified several times.

arguments.description: dumps Proxmark3 flash memory to file
arguments.raw: --dumpmem <dumpfile>                dumps Proxmark3 flash memory to file

arguments.description: starting address for dump, default 0
arguments.raw: --dumpaddr <address>                starting address for dump, default 0

arguments.description: number of bytes to dump, default 512KB
arguments.raw: --dumplen <length>                  number of bytes to dump, default 512KB

arguments.description: raw address mode: dump from anywhere, not just flash
arguments.raw: --dumpraw                           raw address mode: dump from anywhere, not just flash

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# proxmark3 -h
 
 syntax: proxmark3 [-h|-t|-m|--fulltext]
       proxmark3 [[-p] <port>] [-b] [-w] [-f] [-c <command>]|[-l <lua_script_file>]|[-y <python_script_file>]|[-s <cmd_script_file>] [-i] [-d <0|1|2>]
       proxmark3 [-p] <port> --flash [--unlock-bootloader] [--image <imagefile>]+ [-w] [-f] [-d <0|1|2>]
 
 Common options:
     -h/--help                           this help
     -v/--version                        print client version
     -p/--port                           serial port to connect to
     -w/--wait                           20sec waiting the serial port to appear in the OS
     -f/--flush                          output will be flushed after every print
     -d/--debug <0|1|2>                  set debugmode
 
 Options in client mode:
     -t/--text                           dump all interactive command list at once
     --fulltext                          dump all interactive command's help at once
     -m/--markdown                       dump all interactive command list at once in markdown syntax
     -b/--baud                           serial port speed (only needed for physical UART, not for USB-CDC or BT)
     -c/--command <command>              execute one Proxmark3 command (or several separated by ';').
     -l/--lua <lua_script_file>          execute Lua script.
     -y/--py <python_script_file>        execute Python script.
     -s/--script-file <cmd_script_file>  script file with one Proxmark3 command per line
     -i/--interactive                    enter interactive mode after executing the script or the command
     --incognito                         do not use history, prefs file nor log files
     --ncpu <num_cores>                  override number of CPU cores
 
 Options in flasher mode:
     --flash                             flash Proxmark3, requires at least one --image
     --reboot-to-bootloader              reboot Proxmark3 into bootloader mode
     --unlock-bootloader                 Enable flashing of bootloader area *DANGEROUS* (need --flash)
     --force                             Enable flashing even if firmware seems to not match client version
     --image <imagefile>                 image to flash. Can be specified several times.
 
 Options in memory dump mode:
     --dumpmem <dumpfile>                dumps Proxmark3 flash memory to file
     --dumpaddr <address>                starting address for dump, default 0
     --dumplen <length>                  number of bytes to dump, default 512KB
     --dumpraw                           raw address mode: dump from anywhere, not just flash
 
 Examples:
 
 to run Proxmark3 client:
 
     proxmark3 /dev/ttyACM0                       -- runs the pm3 client
     proxmark3 /dev/ttyACM0 -f                    -- flush output every time
     proxmark3 /dev/ttyACM0 -w                    -- wait for serial port
     proxmark3                                    -- runs the pm3 client in OFFLINE mode
 
 to execute different commands from terminal:
 
     proxmark3 /dev/ttyACM0 -c "hf mf chk --1k"   -- execute cmd and quit client
     proxmark3 /dev/ttyACM0 -l hf_read            -- execute Lua script `hf_read` and quit client
     proxmark3 /dev/ttyACM0 -s mycmds.txt         -- execute each pm3 cmd in file and quit client
 
 to flash fullimage and bootloader:
 
     proxmark3 /dev/ttyACM0 --flash --unlock-bootloader --image bootrom.elf --image fullimage.elf
 
 Note (Linux):
 if the flasher gets stuck in 'Waiting for Proxmark3 to reappear on <DEVICE>',
 you need to blacklist Proxmark3 for modem-manager - see documentation for more details:
 * https://github.com/RfidResearchGroup/proxmark3/blob/master/doc/md/Installation_Instructions/ModemManager-Must-Be-Discarded.md
 
 More info on flashing procedure from the official Proxmark3 wiki:
 * https://github.com/Proxmark/proxmark3/wiki/Gentoo%20Linux
 * https://github.com/Proxmark/proxmark3/wiki/Ubuntu%20Linux
 * https://github.com/Proxmark/proxmark3/wiki/OSX

examples.command: proxmark3                                    -- runs the pm3 client in OFFLINE mode
proxmark3 /dev/ttyACM0 -c "hf mf chk --1k"   -- execute cmd and quit client
proxmark3 /dev/ttyACM0 -l hf_read            -- execute Lua script `hf_read` and quit client
proxmark3 /dev/ttyACM0 -s mycmds.txt         -- execute each pm3 cmd in file and quit client

local_help.--help:
syntax: proxmark3 [-h|-t|-m|--fulltext]
        proxmark3 [[-p] <port>] [-b] [-w] [-f] [-c <command>]|[-l <lua_script_file>]|[-y <python_script_file>]|[-s <cmd_script_file>] [-i] [-d <0|1|2>]
        proxmark3 [-p] <port> --flash [--unlock-bootloader] [--image <imagefile>]+ [-w] [-f] [-d <0|1|2>]

Common options:
      -h/--help                           this help
      -v/--version                        print client version
      -p/--port                           serial port to connect to
      -w/--wait                           20sec waiting the serial port to appear in the OS
      -f/--flush                          output will be flushed after every print
      -d/--debug <0|1|2>                  set debugmode

Options in client mode:
      -t/--text                           dump all interactive command list at once
      --fulltext                          dump all interactive command's help at once
      -m/--markdown                       dump all interactive command list at once in markdown syntax
      -b/--baud                           serial port speed (only needed for physical UART, not for USB-CDC or BT)
      -c/--command <command>              execute one Proxmark3 command (or several separated by ';').
      -l/--lua <lua_script_file>          execute Lua script.
      -y/--py <python_script_file>        execute Python script.
      -s/--script-file <cmd_script_file>  script file with one Proxmark3 command per line
      -i/--interactive                    enter interactive mode after executing the script or the command
      --incognito                         do not use history, prefs file nor log files
      --ncpu <num_cores>                  override number of CPU cores

Options in flasher mode:
      --flash                             flash Proxmark3, requires at least one --image
      --reboot-to-bootloader              reboot Proxmark3 into bootloader mode
      --unlock-bootloader                 Enable flashing of bootloader area *DANGEROUS* (need --flash)
      --force                             Enable flashing even if firmware seems to not match client version
      --image <imagefile>                 image to flash. Can be specified several times.

Options in memory dump mode:
      --dumpmem <dumpfile>                dumps Proxmark3 flash memory to file
      --dumpaddr <address>                starting address for dump, default 0
      --dumplen <length>                  number of bytes to dump, default 512KB
      --dumpraw                           raw address mode: dump from anywhere, not just flash

Examples:

  to run Proxmark3 client:

      proxmark3 /dev/ttyACM0                       -- runs the pm3 client
      proxmark3 /dev/ttyACM0 -f                    -- flush output every time
      proxmark3 /dev/ttyACM0 -w                    -- wait for serial port
      proxmark3                                    -- runs the pm3 client in OFFLINE mode

  to execute different commands from terminal:

      proxmark3 /dev/ttyACM0 -c "hf mf chk --1k"   -- execute cmd and quit client
      proxmark3 /dev/ttyACM0 -l hf_read            -- execute Lua script `hf_read` and quit client
      proxmark3 /dev/ttyACM0 -s mycmds.txt         -- execute each pm3 cmd in file and quit client

  to flash fullimage and bootloader:

      proxmark3 /dev/ttyACM0 --flash --unlock-bootloader --image bootrom.elf --image fullimage.elf

Note (Linux):
if the flasher gets stuck in 'Waiting for Proxmark3 to reappear on <DEVICE>',
you need to blacklist Proxmark3 for modem-manager - see documentation for more details:
* https://github.com/RfidResearchGroup/proxmark3/blob/master/doc/md/Installation_Instructions/ModemManager-Must-Be-Discarded.md

More info on flashing procedure from the official Proxmark3 wiki:
* https://github.com/Proxmark/proxmark3/wiki/Gentoo%20Linux
* https://github.com/Proxmark/proxmark3/wiki/Ubuntu%20Linux
* https://github.com/Proxmark/proxmark3/wiki/OSX

if the flasher gets stuck in 'Waiting for Proxmark3 to reappear on <DEVICE>',

commands.name: pm3

arguments.description: this help
arguments.raw: -h/--help        this help

arguments.metavar: / proxmark3 client help the script will forward these options
arguments.raw: -hh/--helpclient proxmark3 client help (the script will forward these options)

arguments.description: list all detected com ports
arguments.raw: --list           list all detected com ports

arguments.description: connect device referred to the N:th number on the --list output
arguments.raw: -n <N>           connect device referred to the N:th number on the --list output

arguments.description: shortcut to use directly the proxmark3 client without guessing ports
arguments.raw: -o/--offline     shortcut to use directly the proxmark3 client without guessing ports

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# pm3 -h
 
 Quick helper script for proxmark3 client when working with a Proxmark3 device
 
 Description:
   The usage is the same as for the proxmark3 client, with the following differences:
    * the correct port name will be automatically guessed;
    * the script will wait for a Proxmark3 to be connected (same as option -w of the client).
   You can also specify a first option -n N to access the Nth Proxmark3 connected.
   To see a list of available ports, use --list.
 
 Usage:
   pm3 [-n <N>] [<any other proxmark3 client option>]
   pm3 [--list] [-h|--help] [-hh|--helpclient]
   pm3 [-o|--offline]
 
 
 Arguments:
   -h/--help        this help
   -hh/--helpclient proxmark3 client help (the script will forward these options)
   --list           list all detected com ports
   -n <N>           connect device referred to the N:th number on the --list output
   -o/--offline     shortcut to use directly the proxmark3 client without guessing ports
 
 Samples:
   ./pm3                       -- Auto detect/ select com port in the following order BT, USB/CDC, BT DONGLE
   ./pm3 -p /dev/ttyACM0       -- connect to port /dev/ttyACM0
   ./pm3 -n 2                  -- use second item from the --list output
   ./pm3 -c 'lf search' -i     -- run command and stay in client once completed

local_help.--help:
Quick helper script for proxmark3 client when working with a Proxmark3 device

Description:
    The usage is the same as for the proxmark3 client, with the following differences:
     * the correct port name will be automatically guessed;
     * the script will wait for a Proxmark3 to be connected (same as option -w of the client).
    You can also specify a first option -n N to access the Nth Proxmark3 connected.
    To see a list of available ports, use --list.

Usage:
    pm3 [-n <N>] [<any other proxmark3 client option>]
    pm3 [--list] [-h|--help] [-hh|--helpclient]
    pm3 [-o|--offline]


Arguments:
    -h/--help        this help
    -hh/--helpclient proxmark3 client help (the script will forward these options)
    --list           list all detected com ports
    -n <N>           connect device referred to the N:th number on the --list output
    -o/--offline     shortcut to use directly the proxmark3 client without guessing ports

Samples:
    ./pm3                       -- Auto detect/ select com port in the following order BT, USB/CDC, BT DONGLE
    ./pm3 -p /dev/ttyACM0       -- connect to port /dev/ttyACM0
    ./pm3 -n 2                  -- use second item from the --list output
    ./pm3 -c 'lf search' -i     -- run command and stay in client once completed

    To see a list of available ports, use --list.

Usage:

commands.name: pm3-flash

arguments.description: Enable flashing of bootloader area (DANGEROUS)
arguments.raw: -b         Enable flashing of bootloader area (DANGEROUS)

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# pm3-flash -h
 Quick helper script for flashing a Proxmark3 device via USB
 
 Description:
   The usage is similar to the old proxmark3-flasher binary, except that the correct port name will be automatically guessed.
   You can also specify a first option -n N to access the Nth Proxmark3 connected on USB.
   If this doesn't work, you'll have to use manually the proxmark3 client, see "/usr/bin/proxmark3 -h".
   To see a list of available ports, use --list.
 
 Usage:
   pm3-flash [-n <N>] [-b] image.elf [image.elf...]
   pm3-flash --list
 
 Options:
   -b         Enable flashing of bootloader area (DANGEROUS)
 
 Example:
    pm3-flash -b bootrom.elf fullimage.elf

local_help.--help:
Quick helper script for flashing a Proxmark3 device via USB

Description:
    The usage is similar to the old proxmark3-flasher binary, except that the correct port name will be automatically guessed.
    You can also specify a first option -n N to access the Nth Proxmark3 connected on USB.
    If this doesn't work, you'll have to use manually the proxmark3 client, see "/usr/bin/proxmark3 -h".
    To see a list of available ports, use --list.

Usage:
    pm3-flash [-n <N>] [-b] image.elf [image.elf...]
    pm3-flash --list

Options:
    -b         Enable flashing of bootloader area (DANGEROUS)

Example:
     pm3-flash -b bootrom.elf fullimage.elf

commands.name: pm3-flash-all
commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# pm3-flash-all -h
 Quick helper script for flashing a Proxmark3 device via USB
 
 Description:
   The correct port name will be automatically guessed and the stock bootloader and firmware image will be flashed.
   You can also specify a first option -n N to access the Nth Proxmark3 connected on USB.
   If this doesn't work, you'll have to use manually the proxmark3 client, see "/usr/bin/proxmark3 -h".
   To see a list of available ports, use --list.
 
 Usage:
   pm3-flash-all [-n <N>]
   pm3-flash-all --list

local_help.--help:
Quick helper script for flashing a Proxmark3 device via USB

Description:
    The correct port name will be automatically guessed and the stock bootloader and firmware image will be flashed.
    You can also specify a first option -n N to access the Nth Proxmark3 connected on USB.
    If this doesn't work, you'll have to use manually the proxmark3 client, see "/usr/bin/proxmark3 -h".
    To see a list of available ports, use --list.

Usage:
    pm3-flash-all [-n <N>]
    pm3-flash-all --list

    pm3-flash-fullimage --list
local_help.-h:
Quick helper script for flashing a Proxmark3 device via USB

Description:
    The correct port name will be automatically guessed and the stock firmware image will be flashed.
    You can also specify a first option -n N to access the Nth Proxmark3 connected on USB.
    If this doesn't work, you'll have to use manually the proxmark3 client, see "/usr/bin/proxmark3 -h".
    To see a list of available ports, use --list.

Usage:
    pm3-flash-fullimage [-n <N>]
    pm3-flash-fullimage --list

binary_metadata.file_type: Bourne-Again shell script, ASCII text executable

apt_metadata.description:
Firmware, flasher, and client for the Proxmark3
This package contains the client and tools for the Proxmark3. It is
dedicated to bringing the most out of the new features for Proxmark3 RDV4.0
new hardware and design but it will also support older hardware revisions.
